value.found_quote: Codex reads `AGENTS.md` files before doing any work. By layering global guidance with project-specific overrides, you can start each task with consistent expectations, no matter which repository you open. ... Codex builds an instruction chain when it starts (once per run; in the TUI this usually means once per launched session). Discovery follows this precedence order: 1. **Global scope:** In your Codex home directory (defaults to `~/.codex`, unless you set `CODEX_HOME`), Codex reads `AGENTS.override.md` if it exists. Otherwise, Codex reads `AGENTS.md`. ... **Merge order:** Codex concatenates files from the root down, joining them with blank lines. Files closer to your current directory override earlier guidance because they appear later in the combined prompt.
value.rationale: The page fully substantiates all key elements of the claim: (1) Codex reads AGENTS.md files before doing any work, (2) instructions are persistent and provide custom guidance, (3) they are layered with a global-then-project precedence and merge order, (4) the instruction chain is rebuilt once per run, and (5) global defaults come from the Codex home directory (~/.codex by default). The page presents this as a current, generally available feature with detailed documentation and examples, indicating an active lifecycle status.
